CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, value) VALUES (6, NULL);
SELECT starts_with(value, 'Abr') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT starts_with(value, 'Abr') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT starts_with(value, 'abr') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3;
SELECT starts_with(value, 'A') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 4;
SELECT starts_with(value, 'Abracadabra') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT starts_with(value, '') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 5;
SELECT starts_with(value, NULL) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 6;
SELECT id, value, starts_with(value, 'Abr') AS begins_with FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, value FROM test_strings WHERE starts_with(value, 'Abr') OR starts_with(value, 'A');
SELECT id, value FROM test_strings WHERE starts_with(value, 'Bra') AND id IN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE value LIKE '%dabra%');
